AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE FERGUSON COMMUNITY NEAR THE END OF THE RUNWAY OF THE LAKE CUMBERLAND REGIONAL AIRPORT. THE TORNADO TRAVELED A HALF MILE THROUGH A RESIDENTIAL AREA CROSSING MURPHY AVENUE NEAR THE GOVER LANE INTERSECTION. SOME ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE WAS NOTED TO STRUCTURES ALONG THE PATH. SEVERAL TREES WERE ALSO DOWNED ALONG THE PATH AND ONE TREE FELL ACROSS AND DESTROYED A SHED. THE TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 65 TO 75 MPH.
SOMERSET PULASKI AIRPORT REPORTED 64 MPH GUST.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON WADDLE STREET IN THE FERGUSON COMMUNITY CAUSING SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO THE CONCRETE WALLS OF A WAREHOUSE BUILDING. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE AND SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED. ESTIMATED MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 100 MPH WERE REACHED. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 700 YARDS.

DAMAGE OCCURRED ON FAIRFIELD AVENUE IN CORBIN. THE ROOF WAS TORN FROM A DOUBLEWIDE TRAILER. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED. AN UPROOTED TREE FELL ACROSS A HOUSE AND CAR. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED OFF. ESTIMATED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE 70 TO 80 MPH. TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A FEW TREES AND LIMBS WERE REPORTED DOWN BETWEEN BROADHEAD AND MOUNT VERNON ALONG OLD US HIGHWAY 150.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES WERE BLOWN DOWN BETWEEN CAMRAGO AND JEFFERSONVILLE BETWEEN 655 AND 705 PM EDT. SEVERAL BARNS WERE ALSO DESTROYED AND HOMES IN THE AREA SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE.

WAR CREEK ROAD THAT RUNS ALONG AND ABOUT 200 FT AWAY FROM WAR CREEK HAS WATER OVER ROAD WASHING OUT THE ROADWAY. NEARBY CREEK SPILLED BANKS AND BEGAN RUNNING OVER JUMP OFF ROAD IN POMEROYTON AT RESIDENCE OF 911 DISPATCHER. REPORTED FLOODING OF YARD AND A GARDEN GETTING WASHED OUT. WATER HAS SINCE RECEEDED...BUT BRANCHES AND DEBRIS WERE STILL REPORTED IN ROAD AS OF 2030 EDT BY ANOTHER TRAINED SPOTTER.

THE FOLLOWING ROADS WERE IMPASSIBLE AND WERE BEING CLOSED BY THE FIRE DEPT.- EAST HIGHWAY 1416 AT WOODS HILL, MARSH CREEK BAPTIST CHURCH ROAD, HAYS CREEK ROAD AND ALL SIDE ROADS LEADING OFF FROM HAYS CREEK ROAD.

WATER IN STRUCTURE ON CORDELL FLATS ROAD. ED MEADOWS ROAD IS IMPASSABLE. ROADS ARE UNDER WATER AT THE INTERSECTION OF LUM STRUNK ROAD AND KY HWY 1470. APPROXIMATELY 15 HOUSE IN THE CORDELL FLATS COMMUNITY HAVE BEEN AFFECTED. THE MARSH CREEK CHURCH IS FLOODED.
WATER WAS RUNNING THROUGH A HOUSE MASH FORK ROAD AT 745 PM. COAL BRANCH ROAD IS CURRENTLY BLOCKED.

FIRE RESCUE REPORTED THAT HIGHWAY 40 WAS CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER AT ABOUT 8 PM EDT. THEY ALSO REPORTED THAT THE INTERESECTION OF HIGHWAY 2019 AND 3334 WAS CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. ALL ROADS ARE NOW OPEN.
ROCK SLIDE ON KY421S NEAR CLAY COUNTY LINE AT 955 PM. ANOTHER ROCK SLIDE WAS ON KY30E AT THE 18 MILE MARKER AT 940 PM. THESE SLIDES HAPPENED DURING THE HEAVY RAIN EVENT.
